content: Current discussions of Self-Refine as a general-purpose LLM refinement framework treat self-feedback as a monolithic single-perspective process, but it can be structurally augmented by integrating the multiple inconsistent solving perspectives from LLM self-reflection mechanisms to create a multi-perspective Self-Refine framework that outperforms both original approaches. This hybrid framework retains the original Self-Refine's key advantage of requiring no extra training or supervised data while addressing its common failure mode of getting stuck in local optima from repeated single-perspective feedback.
